install.note: Ultra-lightweight (<10MB RAM, <1s boot). Run 'picoclaw onboard' after install to initialize.

configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically via ~/.echobird/picoclaw.json -> ~/.picoclaw/config.json. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

first_run.description: PicoClaw uses model_list array format (NOT the deprecated providers object). The config requires: model_list (array of model entries) and agents.defaults.model (default model name).

required_fields.model_list[].model: (string) vendor/modelId format, e.g. 'minimax/MiniMax-M2.7'. Vendor is auto-detected from api_base domain.
required_fields.model_list[].api_key: (string) API key for the LLM provider
required_fields.model_list[].api_base: (string) API endpoint URL, must end with /v1
required_fields.agents.defaults.model: (string) Bare model name (without vendor prefix), e.g. 'MiniMax-M2.7'

common_mistakes: WRONG: using 'providers' object (deprecated, causes 'model not found in model_list' error)
RIGHT: using 'model_list' array with model_name, model, api_key, api_base fields

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Map to PicoClaw config.json as follows:
field_mapping.vendor_detection: Extract vendor from baseUrl domain: api.deepseek.com -> 'deepseek', api.minimaxi.com -> 'minimax', api.openai.com -> 'openai'

steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, apiKey.
2. Create ~/.picoclaw/ directory if it does not exist: mkdir -p ~/.picoclaw
4. Run the merge_script below with the extracted values.
5. Verify: picoclaw agent -m 'Hello, respond with OK' (should print a greeting and exit cleanly)
configure.merge_script:
python3 -c "
import json, os, re

# --- fill from '[Attached models for deployment]' block ---
MODEL_ID   = '<attachment model field>'   # e.g. deepseek-chat
BASE_URL   = '<attachment baseUrl>'        # e.g. https://api.deepseek.com
API_KEY    = '<attachment apiKey>'
# ----------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure /v1 suffix
if not BASE_URL.rstrip('/').endswith('/v1'):
    BASE_URL = BASE_URL.rstrip('/') + '/v1'

# Auto-detect vendor from domain
m = re.search(r'api\.([^.]+)\.', BASE_URL)
vendor = m.group(1) if m else 'custom'

path = os.path.expanduser('~/.picoclaw/config.json')
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)

# Read existing config or start fresh
cfg = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        cfg = json.load(f)

# Remove deprecated 'providers' key
cfg.pop('providers', None)

# Set model_list (replace any existing entry)
cfg['model_list'] = [{
    'model_name': MODEL_ID,
    'model': f'{vendor}/{MODEL_ID}',
    'api_key': API_KEY,
    'api_base': BASE_URL
}]
cfg.setdefault('agents', {}).setdefault('defaults', {})['model'] = MODEL_ID

with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    json.dump(cfg, f, indent=2)

print('Done:', MODEL_ID, '@', BASE_URL, f'(vendor={vendor})')
"

cli.description: PicoClaw uses cli-oneshot protocol for EchoBird integration. The agent itself runs persistently via 'picoclaw gateway'.
cli.role_mechanism: PicoClaw uses AGENT.md file at ~/.picoclaw/workspace/AGENT.md for system prompt. EchoBird writes role content here; PicoClaw auto-detects changes via mtime tracking.
cli.output_format: Plain text with ANSI color codes and structured log lines. Clean response starts after the lobster emoji delimiter. EchoBird strips ANSI codes, filters log lines, removes <think> blocks, and extracts content after the delimiter.
cli.note: Output contains Go structured logs (HH:MM:SS INF/WRN), ASCII art banner, and <think> reasoning blocks. All are automatically filtered by EchoBird.
